failureDiagnosis.description: retailer walked away in round 8. A deal was still possible — this may have been premature.

why: supplier_manufacturer: deal at $104.00 in 4 rounds — ZOPA was $15.00 (seller min $90.00 → buyer max $105.00); seller margin: +$14.00, buyer margin: +$1.00
manufacturer_retailer: retailer walked away in round 8 — ZOPA existed ($4.80 gap) — deal was theoretically possible; last offer was $140.00
issues: [NEGATIVE MARGIN] manufacturer offered $150.00 in round 1 of manufacturer_retailer — margin was -14.80 (below reservation price $135.20). Agent accepted/proposed a loss-making deal.
[NEGATIVE MARGIN] manufacturer offered $145.00 in round 3 of manufacturer_retailer — margin was -9.80 (below reservation price $135.20). Agent accepted/proposed a loss-making deal.
[STALE DATA] 1 agent(s) received stale market prices in manufacturer_retailer, potentially anchoring negotiations incorrectly.
[PREMATURE WALK-AWAY] retailer walked away in round 8 of manufacturer_retailer even though the last offer ($140.00) was within the zone of possible agreement ($135.20–$140.00).
what_to_fix: Constrain manufacturer: do not offer or accept prices that violate your reservation price (margin < 0).
Improve retailer's walk-away logic: only walk away if remaining offers are outside ZOPA, not just below a target.
